Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: User message states prompt with instructions The instructions are about constructing a DuckDB SQL query for processing event logs stored in table event_log and includes columns case_id activity timestamp Process variant is defined as ordered sequence of activities that occur in single case sorted by their timestamp Requirements 1 Construct DuckDB SQL query that determines the sequence of activities per each case using ordering by timestamp 2 Group cases by complete activity sequence to identify unique process variants 3 Count how many cases correspond to each variant and determine top K variants by frequency 4 Return all events from original event log that belong only to cases which are part of these top K variants

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We must output a query that does that using SQL Possibly we can use window function or string_agg with order by to get concatenated sequence for each case_id

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Plan first compute variant for each case SELECT case_id STRING_AGG activity ORDER BY timestamp AS activity_sequence FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id Then join counts per sequence with that derived table

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But the tricky part is top K meaning we need to find variants that have highest frequency a Create a subquery or CTE variants as computed from grouping by case Then rank these based on frequency and filter the top K c Then join back to cases that belong to these variants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Plan using DuckDB SQL query We can use CTEs variant_sequences as computed from grouping by case Then second CTE for ranking variants then final joining event_log with filtered variant list

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The original problem says Return all events from the original event log that belong only to cases which are part of these top K variants So we must return events where case_id is in the set of top K variant cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem says top K variants by frequency that is the number of cases that follow that variant So indeed variant_counts is grouping over variant_sequences aggregated by case_id per variant So we want to filter only events from top K variants using the join with variant_sequences filtered to top variants

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This should be the final query
